root_cause: Three layered bugs prevented web-mode message persistence: (1) The db-web-fallback.ts execute() handler had no branch for 'UPDATE messages SET', causing all updateMessage() calls (which carry final content, tokens, toolCalls) to silently no-op. (2) The POST /api/sessions/{id}/message handler created its own UUID, discarding the client-generated ID — so any subsequent PATCH by the frontend's UUID would never match the stored row. (3) The CORS layer only allowed GET/POST/DELETE, blocking PATCH requests entirely.

crates/ava-tui/src/web/api.rs: Extended AddMessageRequest to accept optional client-provided 'id' field so the server stores the message under the frontend's UUID
Added UpdateMessageRequest struct and update_message() PATCH handler at /api/sessions/{id}/messages/{msg_id} — updates message content in session_manager and persists
Extended MessageSummary struct with optional tool_calls, metadata, tokens_used, cost_usd, model fields for future round-trip fidelity
Fixed all three MessageSummary construction sites to include the new optional fields as None
crates/ava-tui/src/web/mod.rs: Added PATCH to the CORS allow_methods list (was missing PUT and PATCH)
Registered PATCH /api/sessions/{id}/messages/{msg_id} route mapped to api::update_message
src/services/db-web-fallback.ts: Added module-level _msgSessionIndex Map<string,string> that records msgId->sessionId during INSERT so UPDATE can build the PATCH URL
INSERT handler now passes client msgId in the POST body and registers it in _msgSessionIndex
Added UPDATE messages SET handler: extracts the message ID (last param), looks up sessionId from index, calls PATCH /api/sessions/{sessionId}/messages/{msgId} with updated content
Added SELECT from messages WHERE id handler (single-message metadata fetch) returning [] so updateMessage's metadata-merge pre-read doesn't log unhandled query warnings

notes: The backend ava_types::Message struct does not carry tokens_used/cost_usd/model — those are frontend-only fields. The fix persists content updates (which matters for LLM context replay) and returns success for metadata-only updates. Rich metadata (tokens, cost, toolCalls) survives within a single page session via the frontend in-memory store; on session reload from the server only content is restored, which matches the existing Tauri behavior where those fields are stored in the frontend SQLite.
